plugin_desc: Parks PK700-series switch-specific SNMP collection (PARKS-PK700 MIB, IANA PEN 50224). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Parks private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization (cpuUsage), memory utilization (a direct percent-used scalar) and chassis chip temperature (mainChipTemperature). The PARKS-PK700 MIB exposes no fan or power-supply state object, so those are not collected (N/A); per-SFP optical power/temperature are port-level diagnostics, not device health, and are also omitted.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Parks PK700 device control plane (PARKS-PK700 cpuUsage), normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Parks PK700 device, read directly as a percent-used scalar from the PARKS-PK700 MIB. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the main-chip temperature in degrees Celsius of the Parks PK700 device (PARKS-PK700 mainChipTemperature, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across sensors. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
